name: Clause: Negative Polarity
intuition: The gram presents the described event or state as not occurring or not holding. The situation is construed as absent from reality rather than realized.
set_to_1_if: The auxiliary of the target gram is negated with 'not' or the contracted form 'n't' (e.g., 'has not', 'haven't').
The clause contains a negative quantifier in subject or object position (e.g., 'No one has come', 'I have seen nothing').
The adverb 'never' modifies the target gram (e.g., 'has never seen').
The target gram appears in a 'neither…nor' construction expressing negation.
set_to_0_if: A negator occurs in the sentence but does not negate the eventuality expressed by the target gram (e.g., 'I have decided not to go' → the negation applies to 'to go', not to 'have decided').
A negator appears in the sentence but does not negate the target gram (e.g., 'I did not say that he has arrived' → the negation applies to 'say', not to 'has arrived').
examples_positive: I have not seen him. (Direct syntactic negation -> 1)
examples_negative: I have decided not to go. (Negation on embedded verb rather than target verb -> 0)
I didn't know he had arrived. (Negation on matrix verb rather than (embedded) target verb -> 0)
